title: Google I/O 2025 – 7Tnq4y7T4xs
text:
[MUSIC PLAYING] JINGYU SHI: Hi, I'm
Jingyu, and with me is Kate We work on AI on Android. KATERYNA SEMENOVAV:
Android empowers developers like you to
create incredibly smart and engaging experiences. Think about personalization,
context awareness, and new generative tools. Want to build these advanced
capabilities into your app? JINGYU SHI: That's
where Gemini comes in. Today, we'll explore how
Google's powerful Gemini models and APIs help Android
developers achieve this. First, we'll guide you through
intelligent experiences on Android OS powered by Gemini. KATERYNA SEMENOVAV:
Then we will review how you can leverage
Gemini models to implement smart and excellent
experiences into your apps. Let's get started. Android is becoming
even more intelligent, helping users be more productive
and unlock creativity. Gemini, our AI system, is
built into the core of Android. It bridges contacts across apps
to help with task automation and save users time. Gemini can complete actions
across connected apps, like Calendar, Maps,
and many others. For example, you can ask
Gemini to add a reminder or look up information
from an email, and Gemini will complete
the task for you. This identical experience is
available on Android phones, foldables, and tablets,
including the latest Pixel 9 series, Samsung's Galaxy S24
series, and Xiaomi 15 series. And it will be coming
to more devices soon, including form
factors like [? wear, ?] TV, auto, and XR devices. Stay tuned for more
information on how to integrate your own apps with Gemini soon. JINGYU SHI: Now we
talked about how Android OS is more intelligent. Let's talk about
how you can build smart and personalized
experiences with the right Gemini models
and Google's AI in your apps. At Google, we provide
a full tech stack, [? unlocking ?] you to build AI
with the end-to-end experience. This includes our
advanced AI models running in the cloud and our local model
optimized for mobile devices, along with a suite of tools,
runtimes, and infrastructure for AI development.
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – 7Tnq4y7T4xs
text:
[? unlocking ?] you to build AI
with the end-to-end experience. This includes our
advanced AI models running in the cloud and our local model
optimized for mobile devices, along with a suite of tools,
runtimes, and infrastructure for AI development. To navigate these
different options and find the right
solution, let's consider some key questions
for your use case. First, think about modality. Ask yourself, what am I
feeding into the system? Is it text and image, maybe
an audio or a video file? And equally important,
what do I need to generate? Are you looking for text
output or audio stream or perhaps creating an image? Second, consider the
complexity of the task. Does your project require
deep, specialized knowledge in a particular field? Think about things like
medical analysis, legal review, or complex financial modeling. It's that kind of
expert knowledge needed. And third, let's talk
about the context window, the amount
of information you need to process at once. Will you be working with really
large amounts of data, things like analyzing
extensive documents or processing hours of
audio or video files? How much information does it
need to remember or consider simultaneously? That's a lot to consider. So let's look at a
real-world example. I received so many messages
today and need a summary to stay up to date. Here's how I would
do this with AI. Chat messages generally
contain text, image, audio, and video files. But let's start
simple with text. What we want to do is a
straightforward problem-- summarize the text. And text messages
are relatively short, so a model that supports
a moderate context window would be sufficient. This points to
[? our ?] on-device GenAI solution powered
by Gemini Nano. Processing locally right
on the user's phone has several benefits. It increases user data
privacy and safety. Because the data doesn't
have to leave the device, you have much greater
control and security, which is a huge
benefit for users. It provides offline capability.
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – 7Tnq4y7T4xs
text:
It increases user data
privacy and safety. Because the data doesn't
have to leave the device, you have much greater
control and security, which is a huge
benefit for users. It provides offline capability. The feature can work even when
there's no internet connection. And there's no additional
cost for inference every time it runs. These advantages-- privacy
by design, offline access, and cost efficiency at skill
are crucial for features like messages summarization. KATERYNA SEMENOVAV: Gemini Nano,
our most efficient and compact model, is designed and
optimized for running directly on mobile devices. Last year, we launched
experimental access to Gemini Nano, allowing you
to explore its capabilities. We've seen many creative
uses since the launch and heard great feedback
from the developer community. Particularly, there's
a huge interest in platform-level support for
the most common use cases. So we launched the GenAI
APIs powered by Nano allowing you to integrate these
common intelligent experiences into your apps with just
a few lines of code. GenAI APIs are a part
of ML Kit, our Mobile SDK that brings Google's
state of the art on-device GenAI and ML. There are four new generative
APIs already available in beta today. Summarization--
you can summarize texts such as chat
conversations, notes, short articles, or documents. Proofread helps you users polish
short content, like an email. Rewrite-- you can
use this feature to reward different
styles, whether to be more formal, casual, concise,
or with a few emojis. And lastly, image
description-- use this API to provide a short
description of an image. To learn more about how to
integrate gen AI APIs, best practices, and what
we are building next, check out this talk. Besides these APIs,
powered by Gemini Nano, we also provide you with
a set of tools, libraries, including MediaPipe
and [INAUDIBLE] to bring your own custom models. The Google AI Edge
platform allows you to build and deploy a
wide range of custom models on edge devices.
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – 7Tnq4y7T4xs
text:
we also provide you with
a set of tools, libraries, including MediaPipe
and [INAUDIBLE] to bring your own custom models. The Google AI Edge
platform allows you to build and deploy a
wide range of custom models on edge devices. It supports various frameworks
like TensorFlow, PyTorch, Keras, and JAX, enabling
you to integrate AI with more customization
in your applications. Google AI Edge also
supports other AI models. So if you need to customize or
fine-tune a large language model or you want to reach devices
when Nano is not available, you can use other open models
via the MediaPipe LLM Inference API. When serving your
own models on device, one challenge in handling
large model downloads, updates, and the impact on
the user experience. We are making this easier by
launching play for on-device AI. This service is
in beta, making it easy to manage custom model
downloads efficiently. This allows you to deliver the
right model to each Android device exactly
when users need it. You can find out more on the
Android developers website. JINGYU SHI: Now we have explored
what's available on device. What if I want to
analyze and summarize a video file I just
received from my friend or generate an image
and send it as my reply? KATERYNA SEMENOVAV: For more
complex use cases, like audio and video processing, image
generation, or a wider device reach, we recommend using
larger Gemini models running in the cloud. This also includes situations
requiring additional domain knowledge or processing
large inputs. This set of models
include Gemini Flash and Pro, offering a balance
of latency and power for a wide range of tasks. And you can also use the
Imagen model for generating high-quality images. These models excel in scenarios
like creating realistic images or generating longform texts
with deeper understanding, processing large amount
of text, images or video to extract structured
information, answer, complex questions, or
generate insightful summaries, analyzing and reasoning across
different types of data,
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – 7Tnq4y7T4xs
text:
or generating longform texts
with deeper understanding, processing large amount
of text, images or video to extract structured
information, answer, complex questions, or
generate insightful summaries, analyzing and reasoning across
different types of data, understanding the content
of an image or a video and its accompanying text,
building agentic experiences where your app can
proactively perform tasks on behalf of the user by
understanding the intent and leveraging function calling
to interact with other services and APIs-- for example, an app that can
plan a trip, book flights and hotels, all powered by the
intelligence of Gemini models. Integrating these more capable
models into your Android apps is straightforward
through Firebase. Firebase provides a
simplified and secure way to access Gemini Flash,
Gemini Pro, and Imagen models without the need to manage
your own backend service. It supports popular Android
development languages like Kotlin, Java, and even
cross-platform frameworks like Flutter. To test these
model capabilities, you can use Google AI
Studio or Vertex AI Studio that provides direct
access to the models. To learn more on how to
integrate Gemini models into your apps,
check out this talk that deep dives
into the details. JINGYU SHI: OK, let's
bring it all together. When deciding which Gemini
model best fits your use case, it really comes down
to understanding the specifics of your
requirement and model capabilities. If your priority is
offline functionality coupled with budget and
targeting flagship devices, then on-device GenAI is
the solution for you. For common text-based tasks
and where on-device processing and offline capabilities
are beneficial, we encourage you to
build with Gemini Nano [? where ?] the GenAI APIs. On the other hand,
if these requirements are optional but
you need to perform a more complex task requiring
significant processing power, you want to target a
wider device reach, or you need multimodal inputs
or outputs that are not yet supported on
device, we recommend

are optional but
you need to perform a more complex task requiring
significant processing power, you want to target a
wider device reach, or you need multimodal inputs
or outputs that are not yet supported on
device, we recommend building with our more
capable and larger models, Gemini Pro and Flash series, or
the Imagegen model, [? where ?] the Firebase AI SDK. Now you know where to start. Keep in mind, when
releasing into production, you can use a combination
of both on-device and cloud solutions. And we've seen many developers
building innovative experiences using this combined approach. KATERYNA SEMENOVAV:
This session has provided a foundational overview
of exciting possibilities that the Gemini models bring
to Android development. We hope it sparked
your imagination and given you a clear
understanding on how to integrate AI into
your applications. To help you take
the next steps, we encourage you to explore the
following resources linked in the description below. You will find a comprehensive
developer guides and CodeLabs specifically covering
Gemini integration. And we have a sample application
showcasing various Gemini use cases on Android. Ultimately, the key to building
truly impactful Android applications with AI lies in
finding the appropriate model and using the right tools
for your specific needs. JINGYU SHI: By carefully
considering your use case and leveraging capabilities
of the Gemini models, you can build creative,
intelligent, and helpful experiences for your users. We are excited to
see what you will build with the power of Gemini. Thank you for joining us. [MUSIC PLAYING]
speaker: unknown
